title: Simit Sesame Ring Bread

seo.title: How to Make Simit Sesame Ring Bread — Easy Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Simit Sesame Ring Bread. This easy Turkish recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Simit Sesame Ring Bread
easy Simit Sesame Ring Bread recipe
authentic Turkish Simit Sesame Ring Bread

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Turkish Simit Sesame Ring Bread recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Simit Sesame Ring Bread — a beloved Turkish classic

description: Simit Sesame Ring Bread is a beloved Turkish staple, a crusty, sesame-coated bread ring perfect for breakfast or a snack. Its distinct chewy texture and rich sesame flavor make it an irresistible treat enjoyed across Turkey and beyond. This recipe brings the authentic taste of Turkish street food right into your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yeast Mixture
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, active dry yeast, and sugar. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Make the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it is smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Simit
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a long rope, approximately 18-20 inches (45-50 cm) long. Take two ropes and twist them together, then join the ends to form a ring. Repeat with the remaining dough pieces.

instructions.title: Prepare Molasses and Sesame Coating
instructions.text: In a shallow dish, whisk together the grape molasses and 1/2 cup of water. In another shallow dish, spread out the toasted sesame seeds.

instructions.title: Dip and Coat
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Take each simit ring, dip it completely into the molasses mixture, ensuring it is fully coated. Then, transfer it to the dish of sesame seeds, pressing gently to adhere a generous amount of seeds to all sides. Place the coated simit on the prepared baking sheet.

instructions.title: Bake the Simit
instructions.text: Bake the simit in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until they are golden brown and cooked through. The baking time may vary depending on your oven. Once baked, remove from the oven and let them cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: For the best chewiness, ensure your dough is well-kneaded and allowed to rise fully. A warm, draft-free spot is ideal for proofing.

Grape molasses (pekmez) is key for authentic flavor and color. If unavailable, date molasses is a good alternative, but avoid substitutes like honey or maple syrup as they alter the taste significantly.

Simit is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If storing, keep them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days, or freeze for longer storage. Reheat briefly in the oven for a fresh-baked experience.
article: Simit Sesame Ring Bread is a beloved Turkish Baking dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
